context: W5.2 duckduckgo RE-TEST (confirmatory). #1337 verdict FAIL_NOT_RENDERED is NOT a W5.2 regression (confirmed by dev supervisor via engine log): at 02:22:00 the engine blocked duckduckgo correctly — [APF:block] response_type=duckduckgo_sse, [APF:envelope] rendered via DB template, [APF:block_response] size=751 (BYTE-IDENTICAL to today's 3 baselines 18:35/21:36/23:51, all 751), keyword=주민등록번호. duckduckgo uses the duckduckgo_sse SSE path (NOT the WS emit_ws_envelope path migrated in W5.2, which is m365_signalr-only) so W5.2 cannot affect it. The #1337 non-render is the pre-existing h2_end_stream=2 deferred-END_STREAM flush fragility (task #34): on an idle H2 connection the deferred END_STREAM frame is not flushed, so the duck.ai client never receives the complete SSE block response and renders nothing. This is transient/connection-state-dependent (baseline #1316 flushed and rendered; #1337 idle connection did not). This re-test confirms duck.ai renders the 차단 warning on a fresh non-idle connection.
notes: GUI method, duck.ai. duck.ai requires CLICKING the 보내기 (send) button — Enter alone does NOT submit. Steps: hard-reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) to fresh duck.ai SPA, type the PII prompt into the focused editor, click 보내기 send button, then keep the tab ACTIVE/foregrounded and do NOT let the H2 connection go idle while waiting (the deferred END_STREAM #34 fragility drops the block on idle). Wait up to 90s for the duckduckgo_sse block to render the Korean 차단 warning. PASS = '차단' rendered. If blank again, the block is being dropped by the #34 deferred-flush fragility — note that explicitly (engine block is already confirmed correct, size=751). engine block confirmed correct — this is confirming client render only.
